# Tyre Shop / Vulcanizer Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-tyre-shop-tyre-shop-service
**Vertical slug:** tyre-shop
**VN-ID:** VN-COM-AUT-002
**Niche family:** NF-COM-AUT (variant of used-car-dealer)
**Family role:** variant
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's tyre market spans roadside vulcanizers (emergency patching, air, tube replacement) and branded tyre centres (Michelin, Bridgestone, Dunlop authorised dealers). Mushin, Oshodi, and Sango Ota are the hub markets in Lagos; Onitsha and Aba lead in the East. Fleet contracts with transport and logistics companies generate recurring revenue. FRSC enforces minimum tyre tread depth standards for commercial vehicles. The counterfeit tyre problem (substandard imports from China) makes SON conformity certificates a key trust signal. WhatsApp is the standard channel for emergency patch calls and tyre size enquiries.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Tunde, 38, driver-owner (Lagos)**: SUV driver who needs new tyres after puncture. WhatsApps to check stock for 235/55 R18 size and price before visiting. Wants Michelin or Bridgestone.
- **Fleet manager Abubakar, 45, Kano**: Sources tyres for 30-truck haulage fleet. Needs bulk pricing, fast turnaround, delivery capability. Calls dealer or WhatsApps price list.
- **Keke rider Uche, 27, Enugu**: Needs tube replacement and vulcanizing for three-wheeler. Visits roadside vulcanizer but would use WhatsApp to locate one with split-rim service.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (shop name, "Authorised Michelin / Bridgestone Dealer"), WhatsApp emergency CTA, featured tyre grid (6 items with sizes and ₦ prices), trust strip (SON, CAC, brand dealer cert), about blurb
- **About**: Shop history, brand partnerships, FRSC safety compliance, SON conformity
- **Services (Tyre Catalogue)**: Tyre listing by size/brand with ₦ pricing, balancing, alignment, nitrogen inflation, WhatsApp for size request
- **Contact**: WhatsApp emergency call (primary), location (market address), enquiry form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Road-safety-first, practical, and brand-authoritative. "Road-Ready Tyres", "Emergency Patching Available", "Michelin / Bridgestone Authorised". Primary CTA: "Request a Tyre via WhatsApp". Secondary: "View Tyre Catalogue". Avoid: generic western automotive imagery.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- SON (Standards Organisation of Nigeria) conformity certificate
- CAC business registration
- Tyre manufacturer authorised dealer certificate (Michelin, Bridgestone, Dunlop)
- FRSC road safety compliance statement
- Anti-counterfeit guarantee
- Fleet pricing availability notice
- Emergency 24/7 patching service badge

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Tyre fitting in progress — Nigerian mechanic fitting tyre on SUV, workshop setting
2. New tyre display — branded Michelin/Bridgestone stock, price tags in ₦ visible
3. Balancing machine in action — modern equipment, professional setup
4. Fleet tyre delivery — lorry tyres stacked, forklift loading
5. Roadside vulcanizer — classic Nigerian street scene, acetylene torch, split-rim repair
6. SON certificate on wall — framed, authenticity signal
7. Shop signage — branded dealer board, Mushin or Sango Ota market context

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] SON conformity certificate for imported tyres
- [ ] FRSC road safety compliance statement
- [ ] CAC business registration number
- [ ] Brand manufacturer dealer authorisation letter
- [ ] Anti-counterfeit compliance statement
- [ ] NDPR consent on enquiry form

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp emergency tyre CTA prominent
- [x] SON/FRSC trust signals
- [x] Mushin/Oshodi/Sango Ota market reference
- [x] ₦ pricing per tyre size
- [x] Nigerian vehicle types referenced (SUV, salon, bus, truck)
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR on enquiry form
